My other question for you guys before we get started, because the last time I did this presentation, a lot of people were confused about this. How many of you guys know what a funnel is? Some people don't know. And we've got some yeses and some noes. So I just want to really quick recap what a funnel is for those, and then we'll jump and get started. So a funnel, if you step back and you look back at the internet about 10 years ago, this really big thing came out that became really popular and was called a website. I'm sure most of you guys have probably heard of a website. And what's cool is that we would set up a website, and then I would try to put a dollar in advertising into my website. And then the goal, if I did it correctly, I'd get $2 back out. That's kind of the whole, that's the dream. That's the internet dream. Build a website, drive a dollar in ad-zine, get $2 back out. And for 10 years or so, that worked. And it worked really good for me and for tons of other people. We made a lot of money. But the problem is over the last 10 years, the competition's gotten harder. You look at literally these websites popping up all around the world. Millions of websites are popping up every single day. And it's gotten harder and harder and harder. And literally, that exact same website that I used to put a dollar into traffic in, and I try to get $2 back out, I put a dollar in and get nothing out. Or sometimes I get $0.20, $0.30. And I was losing money every single time. And I don't know about you, but as I've been watching what's been happening, it's come to the conclusion that basically the end is near for websites. In fact, in most markets, it's over. It gets done. If you have a website, that used to be good, but it doesn't even work anymore. And it doesn't matter how much you try to do to save this and salvage it. Like literally, you can pay more money or get better design, but a website is dead. I always say that a dead duck is a dead duck. No matter what you try to do to resuscitate it, it's too late. But the good news is that just like how CDs are replaced cassette tapes and DVDs have replaced VHS, there's a thing that's come out to replace websites called a sales funnel. That's kind of what we're going to be talking about today. And the other cool thing is this is kind of what a sales funnel looks like. Now, this may not mean anything to you guys now, but by the end of this, it'll make perfect sense. But a sales funnel is basically, it's a multi-step process. We take your website and you kind of split it up over a couple of steps. And you'll see here in a minute why it works so well. So the last question before we kind of get started, people always ask me, well, do I need a funnel for my business? And I always think back to 10 years ago when people always ask me, hey Russell, I think I need a website for my business. And you think about it now, it's like a no brainer, like well, duh, of course you need a website for your business. And the answer is obviously the same right now. You have to have a funnel for your business. That's how it works today. So does that make sense, you guys? For those of you who don't know what a funnel is, all right, cool. And I built some funnels like I'm going to show you here, that eventually my car payment was paid for. I didn't have to pay a car payment anymore. And I tried something else. And all of a sudden, that bill disappears because that funnel pays for it. And so that was really cool for me. Now, I remember when I was learning this whole internet thing, I was on a tele-seminar one day. And a tele-seminar is what we did before we ever had webinars. So it's been a little while. We're actually had to call on the phone and listen in to somebody. And on this phone call, there was this dude. And he got on and he said that he was an internet millionaire. I remember thinking, wow, how cool would that be to be a millionaire, right? And so I remember I sat down on a piece of paper, I wrote down, I want to be a millionaire. And it seemed pretty cool at first. But I was like, that number's pretty big. I don't know if that's actually realistic. I could be a millionaire. So I'm like, OK, well, if I can't do that, though, what does that actually look like? And so I wrote down $1 million in a piece of paper. And then I did some math. And this is advanced math for me. I divided $1 million by 365. So 365 days a year. And it came out to be $2,739.73 a day. And I said, well, that number's still pretty big. But it was tangible. And I said, look, if I can figure out a simple website, it would make me $20, $30, $40 a day. And then another one, and get the point where eventually I'm making $2,739 a day, I could make $1 million in a year. And so that was kind of my goal. So I started going out there and creating these little websites and these little funnels. And some of them I put out there were complete failures. We didn't make any money at all. And then other ones we put out there were like, I have one that made $20 a day and want to make $80 a day. And they kept going bigger and bigger. And eventually, every once in a while, not every time, but every once in a while, I'd have one that would just knock it out of the park, like the one I'm going to show you guys right now, that literally is making almost $18,000 a day. And so that's kind of my goal. Now, currently in our company, we've got 27 funnels. And each of these funnels make anywhere from $20 a day to $17,947. And if you've ever seen this before, then yes, I'm also the dude who makes money while he sleeps. So that's who Russell Brunson is. All right, so I'm ready to jump into this. And what I want to show you guys is kind of how I got started in this whole funnel concept. This is a picture here of my very first funnel. And I put funnel in quotation marks because it wasn't really a funnel. All it was was basically a website. And I had a website and in order form, that was it. This is my very first product. I'm from Boise, Idaho. And if you ever come to Boise, you'll notice there's potatoes everywhere we're famous for. And so for fun, we go in and we make potato guns. And so this is my very first info product I put out there. And we started selling it. And it was just fun to see people give me money for something I invented and kind of put out there. And what was cool about it is this is one of those little funnels I was talking about earlier that was making me about $20 or $30 a day. Now a little while later, I had four or five of these little projects like that. And one day, one of my friends called me up. And he said, hey, Russell. And he was doing the same thing as me. He had little websites. And he was doing the same thing. And he called me one day and said, hey, Russell, check this out. We started adding these things onto all of our websites called One Time Offers, OTOs. He says, basically, I can upsell. So somebody buys my first product. And they said that a percent of those people will then buy my upsell. And he said, sure enough, I'm getting like 20% to 30% of people who buy my first thing to buy my next thing. I was like, are you serious? That sounds really, really cool. I want to try that out. So I went back to all my little tiny websites. I went to add these little upsells. And they were just simple, like one little page with a little video of me saying, hey, buy this other product. And typically, the product was more expensive than the front end. And sure enough, just like he said, I was getting 20% to 30% of people who bought my first product, then they come and buy the second product. And so for me, because the upsell was more expensive, that literally doubled my business. Just by adding this little, tiny page onto each of my little websites. That was the beginning of a funnel for me. A funnel is like one product. And then you have an upsell and things like that. So this is my very first funnel. And it was just amazing to me that by adding one little tiny page, I could double how much money I made. You guys getting this? Just that one little tiny tweak. And so for me, I don't know about you, but when I realize I could double how much money I can make by adding one little page, I got obsessed. I wanted to try all sorts of things. Now, for most people, if you want to double how much money you're going to make, what do you have to do? If you were a doctor and you want to double your income, you got to go like 10 more years of school, right? For me, I just added one little tiny page. So I thought, that was awesome. I'm going to do it again. So I started adding more pages. And I started testing upsells and downsells and exit pops and a bunch of different things. And just to see what would work and what worked better and what worked worse. And after a while, we invented this kind of funnel that we just had kind of invented called the microcontinuity funnel. And it was just a new way that we had kind of built out with different pages and different orders. And it worked awesome. And remember, I shared this with some people. And we had thousands of people who started making microcontinuity funnels and had tons of success. And it's based on some of these tests we were doing. And then a few years later, one of my friends called me up. He said, hey Russell, I think I invented a new type of funnel. He said, I called this an automated evergreen webinar funnel. And he showed me how it worked. He said, first thing I have is a registration page. And after some of the registers, then there's a confirmation page. And after they go through that, then here's the email sequence. And he mapped out what this funnel looked like. I remember looking at it and thinking, wow. I've never in a million years would have dreamt that that would actually work. And so I said, hey, do you mind if I model this? Like, I want to make one just like you did. He said, yeah, sure. So I went back and so I made my own automated webinar. I had a registration page that matched his. I had a confirmation page. And I modeled the look and the fill and the layout and the sequencing. I did everything as close as I could to his. And I launched my very first automated webinar. And I didn't know what to expect. I knew what he had done well with his, but I didn't know what to expect. And I launched it. And in the first 90 days, we did over a million dollars worth of sales. Can you imagine that? And I know for most of you guys, you're not going to make a million dollars worth of sales in your first automated webinar. And I would have thought I was going to either. But because I modeled what he did, because I copied the step by step process he had done, I was able to have success just like he did. All right, so it's pretty cool. Luke's giving me some exclamation points. Marcus can imagine. So cool. All right, so that was an automated webinar. Now again, as you can probably tell, when I started learning that these little tweaks would double my business, and by setting up pages in a certain way, I could make a million dollars, all these things I became obsessed. We started testing everything. We started testing the landing pages and the emails. And it's trying to see what would work. And about a year ago, we actually launched a product called 108 Proven Split Test Winners. And this book was every split test, every test we had tried, we found out what worked the best. And we published them in this book. And initially, I was going to sell this book for a few thousand dollars, because I thought it was worth that. And then we decided, and you said, most people are out there trying to learn this stuff. And it takes them a long time to learn it and to implement. I said, what if we just gave them this book? Like, this is like a cheat sheet. Instead of them testing for two years on what landing pages are going to work the best, they can open our book and just copy the one in there. And so we decided to do that. And in the last year, we sold over 10,000 copies of it and seeing thousands of people's businesses change almost overnight because of it. And so for me, this presentation and everything we do as a company is all about that. Like, how can I give you shortcuts? How can I give you hacks? They're going to shortcut your process to success. And so that's kind of what Funnel Hacks is all about. Now, over the last 10 years of doing this, I've done a ton of things right. But I've also done a whole bunch of things wrong. And so my goal for you guys is I don't want you doing the wrong things. I want you guys to come straight to the successful part. Because I don't want you guys to be successful in a six months from now. I don't want you to be successful in a year from now. I want you guys to be successful now. So that's kind of my process. Now, I want to show you guys, earlier I talked about one of our Funnels that's currently making almost $18,000 a day. And this is a picture of it right here in my office. Can you guys all see that? So this is one of my weird niche Funnels. And I call it a weird niche Funnel because in this Funnel we're selling a supplement helping people with neuropathy. Now, when I started building out this Funnel, I didn't even know what neuropathy was. But I knew there was a market and demand there, so we started creating it. Now, this is the hard way. Now, we built this Funnel about a year ago. And the way we did it was really hard. In fact, if you've ever tried to build a sales funnel, it's probably the way you did it in the past. There's not an easy way to do it. And so this is the way that we did it. Now, I'm going to walk you through the process. One thing I want to make sure you don't get overwhelmed because this is not the way we do it anymore. I'm going to show you guys after this a really simple and easy way. But this is the way that we literally had to do it for 10 years. So the first step for making this Funnel is how to design the funnel. Now, for me, that's my favorite part, to sketch out like, what's the pages going to look like? Where's the upsells and the downsells? And how do all the pieces kind of work together? Now, after that, I had to hire somebody to write the sales letter. So all the words and all the pages, I had to hire someone to do that. So I wanted a really good copywriter, so I hired one of the best. And he charged me $20,000 for a sales letter. It's kind of a lot of money, but that's what it costs to get a really good sales letter done. After the sales letter was done, then I went out there and the copywriter basically gave me back a Word document, a whole bunch of words on it. And I was like, this isn't a website. So then I hired a designer. He took the words and made all the words look really, really cool, to the link and look like a website. Then he was done. He sent me over some image files, said, here's your website. And I was like, these are image files. It's not an actual website yet. So then I had to go and hire a website designer to take these images, to slice them up and get them up online. So now they're on an actual website, so I'm like, cool. I'm almost there. Then I went to try to buy, and the order form didn't work. I'm like, great. So then I had to go hire a programmer to connect the order forms and the shopping carts, and the one click upsells and all those pieces. Then I had to hire an analytics person to set the split test. So what I always said and done for me to set up this one single funnel, my upfront costs were $29,000. Can you guys imagine that? $29,000. That's the equivalent of a brand new car, right? But instead, I sent $29,000 getting what? One, two, three, four, five, six, six pages. But the worst thing for me was not the upfront costs. The worst thing for me is it took us between six and eight weeks to create this. I had already bought the supplement. I had a whole shipping warehouse full of these supplements, and it took me two full months to get this whole thing put together to be able to start selling it. And that was the worst thing for me. Now again, like I said, this is the way that our company, and most people have been doing this for the last 10 years, and it's hard, it's expensive, it takes a lot of time. And it's no wonder that people struggle to make money online, right? Because if you have to go through all this stuff, it's not easy. How many of you guys would like to learn how to do that? Yeah, it's way easier just to get them to come to your website, I promise you. Cool, so that's what we're going to cover. So I'm going to jump right in. All right, so secret number one, how to ethically steal over a million dollars worth of funnel hacks from your competitors for under $100. Now to understand this process, the first thing you got to understand is this concept I learned from Tony Robbins. He said, if you want to achieve success, all you need to do is find a way to model those who've already succeeded. It makes sense, right? So I started thinking back about when I was growing up and the things I was doing. So when I was young, I was a wrestler, and I wanted to be the best wrestler in the entire country. But when I got started, I wasn't very good. I didn't know what I was even doing. So I went out there and I started looking at who are the people that are super successful, who are the people that are doing what I want to be doing. I looked at what they were doing, and I modeled them. I modeled the moves, I modeled what their training regimens were, as much as I could. I modeled what they were doing to be successful, and guess what happened? Yeah, I became successful. I was a state champ, I was a national American, I got a college scholarship, because I modeled who was successful. Now when I started my business, I don't know about you guys, but I was not like a business person, right? I had no business degree, I didn't know anything about business. All I knew was I wanted to make some money, OK? And that's how I think a lot of us entrepreneurs get started, is we just want to make some money. And so I got online, and I'm like, well, I don't even know anything about anything, but I see all these people having success. What if I just modeled what they were doing? So I looked at people that are having success, and I modeled it, and people always tell me, Russell, how in the world did you get successful so fast online? It's because I just looked at what other people were doing, and I did the same thing. I did my own products, but I modeled what they were doing. Now the exact same thing is true with a sales funnel. If you want to be successful, you've got to find a sales funnel of somebody else that's already successful, and then you model it. Does that make sense? Now every single year, we've got a high-end coaching program, and every month or so, somebody will come in and say, Russell, guess what? I've got it all figured out. I figured out this new market that nobody's in yet. It'll be the very first ones. We're going to make billions of dollars, and they're so excited. And I usually kind of smile for a second. I step back and I say, look, you can always tell who the pioneers are because they have arrows in their back, and they're lying face down in the dirt. And they always kind of look at me from the other side. I said, look, this is the deal, you guys. I want you to be successful as quick as possible. And sure, you can go out there, and you can try to trail blaze, but the problem with trailblazing is that you get arrows in your back. I said, I want to follow people who've already been successful, who already have those arrows, who've already done the hard work, and proven that something works, and then just model them. Now, for example, I had a chance to go to a seminar a few years ago, and there was this guy that got up and spoke. His name was Porter Stansbury. And Porter worked for a company called Agora Publishing. Now, if you've never heard of Agora, they are one of the biggest info publishers in the world. They publish financial newsletters, and they sell almost a billion dollars worth of info products a year, a billion with a B. And Porter got up there, and he was talking about their business, and he actually runs the biggest division of Agora. It's called Stansbury Research. And he was talking about what they do in their marketing and stuff, and it was really cool to see. And then he stopped, and he said, you know what's interesting? He says, every year, we get competitors who see how much money we're making, and they want to come and try to compete with us. He says, the first thing they always do is they come, and they try to undercut us with pricing. So they try to come in cheaper than us. And then they come back, and they try to do more expensive. They try this. They try all these different things to try to beat us. He says, do you not realize that we are Agora publishing? He said, we spend hundreds of millions of dollars every single year testing everything you can dream of. Price points, upsells, downsells, colors, schemes, everything you can dream of. He's like, we've tested it all. He said, if you want to be successful in the financial newsletter market, this is the only price point that'll work. This is the only upsell that'll work. This is what it has to look like. He says, if you want to compete with us, you have to model us. And I remember sitting back and thinking, wow. Like, think about that. He's literally got hundreds of millions of arrows in his back. Why would I go and try to do it myself? I'm going to look what they've done and start at success. Now, for you guys to be able to really understand this process, the first thing you need to understand is that for any business that you're in or the business you want to be in, there are two types of competitors. First are your direct competitors. So anyone who's selling the exact same product that you have, and the second are your indirect competitors. So an example of a direct competitor for me, like I said, we sell a nerve pain supplement. So anyone else selling nerve pain supplements is my direct competitor. Then my indirect competitors are people who aren't selling the same product as me, but they're selling to my same target market. So for example, I found out with my nerve pain supplement that about 85% of my audience are diabetics. And so anyone who's selling any kind of diabetic product is my indirect competition. So diabetic cookbooks or testing strips, whatever it is, those are my indirect competitors. So for me, if I want to be successful, first thing I have to know, I've got to find out exactly who my direct competitors are, exactly who my indirect competitors are, because I need to know what they're doing. I've got to see what their sales funnels look like. So that's step number one. Now after you've done that, the first thing I do is go out there and you actually model your competition. You model their layout. So when we were on to your nerve pain supplement, we started doing some research and looking around, and we found these guys right here. And they were selling a very similar product to us, and they were pioneers. They were the only ones we were able to find at the time. They'd gone out there. They'd gotten all these arrows in their back. And I don't know exactly how much money they were making, but based on what we can kind of guess from traffic trends and things like that and what they're doing, they're making about $100,000 to $200,000 a month. So I'm like, OK, that's not too bad. That's a cool business to start. And so when I was getting started, I had two options. One is try to go be creative, be an entrepreneur, and try to make my own thing. Or number two, say, look, these guys have proven what works. They know what this market responds to. Let's just model what they're going to do. So that's what we did. And our very first version of ourselves pages looked like this. Now notice I said model. I didn't say copy. This is the ethic piece of it. I'm not trying to ethically steal anything. I'm just modeling the look and the feel in the land. I'm not copying their words. I have my cells that are my everything, but I'm modeling the look and the feel. So they have a logo on the left-hand side. I got a logo. They got a phone number on the right-hand side. I got a phone number. They have a long-form cells that are I have a long-form cells that are. They got a picture of their bottle on the right-hand side. I got a picture. So I modeled the look and feel as close as I possibly could because I knew that it worked. And when we launched this initially, we got the point where it was making about $20,000 a month. So not too bad, right? After we ran that for a little while, I said, well, they're making more money. Like, what else are they doing? I've got to figure this out. And so I did something that every good entrepreneur has to become really, really, really good at doing. I went to their website. I pulled my credit card out of my wallet, out of my pocket, pulled my credit card out of my wallet, and I went to the website and I typed in my credit card number and I bought their product. It cost me, I don't know, $40, $50. I bought their product to see what their upsell flow looked like. And I found out these guys, they're not very advanced marketers. They didn't have an upsell or downsell or anything. I said, OK, crap. Well, they don't really have a funnel. There's got to be someone out here in this market that's killing it does have a funnel. So then I started going out there looking for some of my indirect competitors. So I started looking for a bunch of them. And eventually, I stumbled upon a site that, based on, I had some friends that were doing traffic for these guys. And so I was able to find out what their numbers were. But they were doing between $2 and $3 million a month. I was like, wow, these guys know what they're doing. And so I went to their website and just kind of see what was happening. And I went to the first page and they had a video sales letter like this. I clicked on the Add to Cart button and it had an order form like this. And I looked at it close. And it's OK, they have one, three, and four bottles. Here's their price points. And then I did that thing that every good entrepreneur has to do. I pulled my credit card out of my wallet, typed in the digits, and I bought his product. Now what happened next completely shocked me. And to this day, it still doesn't make any sense to me. But there was an upsell. And his upsell was six more bottles of the exact same thing I just bought. I remember being so confused thinking, I just bought four bottles of this. Why would I buy six more bottles? I remember thinking, this guy's a moron. Why would he ever do that? And I almost just wrote it off as like he wasn't paying attention. And luckily for me, I sat back and said, you know what? He's doing $2 to $3 million a year. Maybe I'm just going to close my mouth and just model what he does. And just assume that he's got some arrows in his back. And I'm just going to just model him. So I did that. Then I went through the rest of his page, he had some more upsells and downsells. And so this was his sales funnel. He was doing $2 to $3 million a month. So I went here looking at everything. I looked at the price points, how many options, how many bottles, everything in it. And I said, OK, this is what's working. I'm going to model it. So I came back now to our sales funnel. And then we modeled it. So I created a new page that looked very similar to his. I had a video sales letter on it. I add the cart button. When you click on that, it came to an order form. We had one, three, four bottles just like him. When someone bought that, we had an upsell of six more bottles. And I just followed everything to a tee. And we modeled the look and the fill and the layout and the price points and everything as close as we could. We took it out there and launched it. And when we did, we went for making $20,000 a month to almost $20,000 a day. He was getting this. That's how you scale a company quickly. It's not by guessing and trial and error and all those things. It's by finding the pioneers who have the arrows in the back, who spent the millions of dollars to find a proven system, and then you just model it. Now the coolest thing about this, you guys, for me, I don't know about you, but I could have gone to Harvard MBA school. I could have hired coaches and consultants, a whole bunch of stuff, and it would have cost me a ton of money. But the reality is all I had to do to figure out what he was doing was invest about $100. Not even that. It was under $100. I was looking at the comments right here. And we got, Mark is to say, OMG, this is crazy. Luke said, mind blown. This is reality, guys. This is how it works. Can we find what's already working and then we model it? Now step number three in the process, this is where us as entrepreneurs, we get a chance to try to be creative. You don't do it in a step one or step two. Wait until you make like $20,000 a day, then you can be creative. So step three, this is where I had a chance to try to figure out if I could do better than what we already had. So I took our control. This was a page that we kind of model. I said, OK, what are the ideas do I have? And we tried a bunch of things. And eventually after a couple of months, we built this version of the page that is now our current control. And this is the one that's winning. But notice, I didn't have a chance to be creative and try to figure stuff out until step number three. If you try that step number one, again, that's when you start getting arrows in your back. And most people run out of time and money before they ever make any money at all. Now I want to show you guys a really quick video from one of my friends, his name is Jamal. He runs a company called FitLife TV. And they were about to launch a new supplement. And they wanted to hire me to help them consult and kind of give them some feedback. And so I'm going to show you guys a video really quick. And I know that we're on go to webinar. It's going to be choppy. You're not going to be able to see it very well. But I want you to listen to the audio of what he says, because this is going to really drive home the point of what we're talking about. So really quick backstory. I had a chance to go out and spend a little while with these guys in their company. And they were about to launch a new supplement. And they're like, hey, look at our funnel. What should we do? And I was like, just copy mine. It works really, really good. So I'd love for you to kind of talk about what happened when you guys did your funnel and then some of the results that have happened since then. Yeah. I'd like to re-emphasize his point about just cloning and copying and modeling what works. So we are a digital product company. We have information products, coaching products. And we were doing about a million a year. And I manage all the sales funnels and sales optimization. And we were going to come out with the physical product line. And we were like, who's done this before? Who's been really successful? We reached out to Russell. He helped us model exactly in our cell. Actually, it's funny because I'm looking at what you modeled. And I had the exact same reaction. The first upsell that we have in place is more of the same thing that they just bought. And we were like, that doesn't make any sense. So we sold them a bottle of a new product called Organifi. Our first one-time offer was three more bottles after they already bought that. And the take rate was like 20% or 30%. Then we thought, Russell actually mentioned, we should implement subscription on everything. So we had three options. We modeled exactly Russell's first sales page. One bottle, three bottles, four bottles. Modeled his pricing, $47, $129, $149. Exactly the same. Sorry for stealing everything. But it works. I love it. It's still away. Yeah. And then once we implemented all the pieces, in actually like a week, which is crazy. We matched his results. We're doing, am I allowed to say the numbers? Yeah. I hope so. I want to hear. Yeah. Who wants here's numbers? Yeah. I want to hear his numbers. Let's hear him. So the first day that we opened up the gates, we did 12,000 in sales. The second day, we did 34,000. The third day, we did 36,000. And that was just opening the gates. And every single day after that, we've done around 15,000 to 20,000. Because of the way that the funnels are structured. So it's really exciting. And we owe a lot to Russell. I mean, he came out. He helped us with our funnels. He broke down everything we were doing wrong, everything we were doing right, and showed exactly what they did. And we just copied it. And it works. So that's awesome. Give him a round of applause. Thank you, Russell. So thank you, Russell. Yeah. All right. So you guys getting how this whole process works, right? You think about that, like, a lot of times, there's no way I'm going to be a success the first time out of the gate. But you look at Jamel and look what these guys did. Like, they modeled something successful. And they almost matched our numbers, number for number from day number one, which is exciting. I don't care who you are. It's exciting. Now, people always ask me afterwards, well, Russell, that's awesome. But I don't have a supplement funnel. I don't have any supplements. And so I want to stress that this is not just work for supplements. It does work for supplement funnels, but also works for coaching funnels, for info product funnels, for e-commerce funnels. And if you happen to have a real business, I have friends who have real businesses. They always tell me my internet ones are fake. But if you've got a real business, like an offline business, it'll actually work for you as well. This works for all types of funnels. This is an e-commerce. Now I've never been known as an e-commerce guy. And one day one of my friends came out to me and he was selling this website. And it was this little cold sore zapper. And he started using this process. And he started building out a funnel. And he had this really cool process for generating leads in his local area offline. And he said, let me try to model that online. So he built this little funnel where he gives away free lunch. He has people come in. He does the presentation. He closes them. He launched this thing. And almost from day number one, he built this amazing lead generation machine happening on Facebook. So if you guys have a real business, you might want to model Chad's funnel here. Because it's killing it for him for generating new leads into his practice. Now, people always ask me these two questions. Like, well, Russell, that's cool. But where do I find a good funnel? And the second thing is, after I find that funnel, how do I know how much money these guys are actually making? Both are very good questions. Let me walk you through it. So the first thing I do, if I want to find a successful funnel, I go to a website called clickbank.com. Now, clickbank is cool because they have over 100,000 successful funnels. In every market you can dream of, in fact, one of my friends is making about 10 grand a day with a funnel on clickbank selling how to make your own chicken coop. And I didn't know that was even a real thing. I thought it was a joke when he first told me. And I saw his numbers. We're making over 10 grand a month using a funnel on how to make your own chicken coop. So I'm guessing that whatever business you're in, there's probably a successful sales funnel on clickbank that you can model for that. So I go to clickbank.com. In the very top, there's this little button that says Marketplace. Do you guys see it there? You click on Marketplace, and then boom, it'll show you all of the sales funnels. Now, two really cool things. First off, it lists the sales funnels based on which ones are selling the most right now. And it also breaks it down into different categories. So for this one, I clicked on Health and Fitness. And I looked out here in the very top product, one that's selling the most, is a website called the Venus Factor. So this is the number one health product inside of clickbank right now. So if I was going to go and launch my own health product, I'd probably go to this site right here. I'd find Venus Factor. I'd go and I'd look at their sales funnel. I'd probably go buy their product. I want to see what they're doing, right? Now, people always say, well, how much money do you think? How do you know how much money these guys are making? So I have no idea what Venus Factor is making, but my guess is there's somewhere in the ballpark at $40,000 to $50,000 a day. And for me, this is really cool because in the past, I would ask my website designers to change the order form and to move things around. And literally when they would try to do that, they would just break the order form. And it was just, it was so painful and so many mistakes that we just stopped doing it. We're now like, someone like me, I can go drag and drop and move things around and just magically it works, it never breaks and it's pretty awesome. So there is the order form. Now, if you have your own shopping cart, we integrate with most of the major shopping carts so you can plug them in. If you don't have a shopping cart, that's fine. Because click funnels also has a built-in shopping cart. So you can just go, click couple buttons, you add in a stripe account and I'll walk you that later and boom, you got your entire order process here. All right, so here's the one time offer. This is the first page of my funnel where I'm gonna start doing upsells. Now before, if you wanna do upsell like in Infusionsoft for example, I remember when I was using Infusionsoft, I had to hire someone and they charged me $2,500 to set up a one-click upsell. Is that crazy? But inside click funnels, you just click a button and boom, one-click upsell magically works. All right, so the order form is done, the upsell is done. So I type in venusfactor.com right here, I click search, check this out. It shows exactly how many visitors they're getting every single month. So if an ad's been running for 728 days, what do I know? I know there's a crap ton of people, it works, right? So I modeled the look and the feel and the size and everything as close as I could because we know it works, right? And then what I do after I have a banner ad done, I go back to the exact same websites that Venus Factors buying ads on and I buy ads on the exact same websites. So they have to sell through a lot of different advertisers. The premise of this movie is Leonardo DiCaprio, he needs to get people to do stuff, right? And so what he does is he goes back inside people's dreams and he goes and he places a thought or an idea inside someone's brain, inside their dream, and he gets somebody to gravitate and to take that as their own idea. And if they get that as their own idea, he's caused Inception, where now when they wake up they're more likely to do that thing that he needs them to do, right? Now for me, selling online is the exact same way, okay? We know that people hate to be sold, but we also know that people love to buy. So ClickFunnels hosts your pages, but it doesn't mean that your page has to look like it doesn't say ClickFunnels in the URL. Okay, so good question there. All right, more questions coming in. So here, who owns the data? Okay, this is a good question, who owns the data? So when somebody comes in and they join your list, they buy your product, whatever it is, that person's yours. We don't touch that, I don't own that, I don't even know who that, that data that people, it's 100% yours, so don't worry. It would be unethical and illegal for me to do anything with it, so I'm not gonna ever email your list or send your customers anything, those customers are all yours, don't worry. Good questions. Can I export my pages? So inside of ClickFunnels, there's not a button like to click and export all your pages, because inside of ClickFunnels, there's all the split testing and the multi-sequence steps and all the stuff, and so if you just, if we give you about export your pages, they wouldn't really work. Obviously you can have a webmaster go back and fix them, so we don't have an export button, but if you really, if you want your pages like a backup of them or whatever, if you go to any page and you right click on it and save as, it'll save the entire page, the way that ClickFunnels is set up, and this is kind of techy, nerdy talk, but all the links are absolute and all the page URLs and stuff and all the images, so you can basically export it and boom, it'll work just as is, which is pretty cool. Next question, do I need WordPress now? So a lot of people ask this because I know that there's plugins that try to make WordPress create funnels, and I want to say something, WordPress is a blogging platform, it was created for people to blog on it, it was never, ever, ever created to build sales funnels, okay? Now there's some really smart markers that have gone back and they've created plugins and stuff to try to hack together WordPress to make it build a sales funnel, the problem is it wasn't made for that, so it starts working and you get it to work, it takes a lot of effort and hard work, and then you get it to work, and the next thing happens, WordPress updates something and the whole thing crashes. I had a friend who had an entire sales funnel on WordPress, left town for the weekend, was gone for four or five days, came back, it turns out for four or five days his entire sales process has been down, he'd been driving traffic through Facebook, he lost tens of thousands of dollars because he used WordPress, so if you want to use WordPress, it's really good for blogging, use WordPress to blog, do not ever use WordPress for sales funnels, it's crazy, it wasn't made for that, ClickFunnels is made for funnels, WordPress is made for blogging, so you'll only need WordPress if you have a blog, if you're not blogging, then just get rid of it, okay, you do not need it, there's no purpose for it. Our next question here, do I need an auto responder? So yeah, ClickFunnels has a basic email auto responder in place, so like when somebody registers for a webinar, it can send an email sequences, it can also do really cool smart triggering so that if somebody leaves early, they get a different email sequence, but if you want to be able to do broadcasts and things like that, you will need an email auto responder, so I usually recommend Aweber or GetResponse, that's the only other tool you really need on top of ClickFunnels.
